hooks.name: Security Review on Credential Edits
hooks.description: Mirror of the uk-translation-review hook, but for credential-handling code. When a secret backend, credential resolver, or password file is edited, it invokes the security-reviewer sub-agent to verify SecretString usage, zeroization of intermediates, stdin pipes for CLI passwords, and the absence of secrets in logs/errors. Forms a 'verification loop' so security invariants are checked automatically instead of relying on memory.

action.prompt: A credential-handling Rust file was just edited. Invoke the security-reviewer sub-agent to audit the change. Use invokeSubAgent with name "security-reviewer" and a prompt like: "Review the recently edited credential file(s). Verify: (1) passwords/keys use secrecy::SecretString, never plain String; (2) intermediate expose_secret().to_string() values are wrapped in zeroize::Zeroizing; (3) CLI passwords are passed via stdin pipe, never as process args; (4) no secret values appear in tracing logs, error messages, or Debug output. Report any violation with file and line; if clean, say so briefly." Report the sub-agent's findings. If it reports no issues, a one-line confirmation is enough.
